name: Burnham's Assistant

choices: Show Burnham the mismatched signature on the manifest
Ask Burnham what he already suspects
Request permission to go directly to the freight yards
opening.narrative:
Chicago, May 1893. The White City rises from the lakefront mud, its plaster palaces gleaming in the morning haze — ten thousand workers still swarming Jackson Park in the final days before the Exposition opens. But beneath the spectacle, something is wrong. A tension moves through the corridors of the Administration Building that has nothing to do with last-minute preparations.

You are assistant to Daniel Burnham, Director of Works. When he needs eyes he can trust, he sends for you.

This morning, a shipping manifest crossed your desk: electrical equipment, diverted from its declared route. The signature authorizing the change does not match any name on Burnham's staff.

Burnham's office door stands open. He is at his desk, jaw set, a telegraph slip folded in his hand. He looks up.

npcMoments.text: 'Shut the door. Tell me what you found in those papers.'

briefing.who: You are Daniel Burnham's trusted aide, authorized to move through the Administration Building and question fair staff without raising suspicion.
briefing.stakes: If the sabotage succeeds on opening day, the failure belongs to Burnham — and to you.
briefing.mission: Trace a diverted shipment of electrical equipment back to whoever forged the transfer order before the Exposition opens in three days.

represents: Administrative staff working under Daniel Burnham at the World's Columbian Exposition, Chicago, 1893
description: A trusted aide with access to Burnham's office and the Administration Building. You move in official circles and can question staff without raising suspicion.
perspective: The player is Burnham's assistant. Burnham is the player's superior and may speak to the player directly, give orders, and act as a boss. Write the player as an aide acting on Burnham's authority.
bridge_sentence: The blueprints on the drafting table are still pinned down.
context_sentence: "You are a senior administrative aide to Daniel Burnham, Director of Works for the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition, whose 690-acre fairground on the Chicago lakefront required coordinating more than 40,000 workers and an unprecedented network of electrical infrastructure to bring the White City to life on schedule."
